title: Firmus Nantz

{{Quote|Control space and you control any battlefield you can imagine. Unfortunately, you cannot control space.|Admiral Firmus Nantz|<ref name="EGTW" />}}
[[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] '''Firmus Nantz''' was a [[New Republic Defense Fleet/Legends|New Republic Fleet]] [[Commander/Legends|commander]]—and later senior flag [[Officer/Legends|officer]] of the [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|New Republic Defense Force]]—during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. From [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] to [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]], Nantz commanded the [[New Republic First Fleet|First Fleet]], and oversaw the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] campaign to liberate the [[Western Reaches/Legends|Western Reaches]] from the rule of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the various Imperial [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] states that had arisen in the wake of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Death/Legends|death]] at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. His campaigns blunted or curtailed the plans of several of the new warlords, including [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Utoxx Prentioch]], [[Superior General]] [[Sander Delvardus]], and Moff [[Par Lankin]]. During this [[Time/Legends|time]], Nantz was responsible for recruiting [[General/Legends|General]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] to form a [[Lando's Commandos|unit]] to deal with [[Eiattu pirates|rogue Imperial pirates]] plaguing New Republic shipping. By [[16 ABY/Legends|16 ABY]], Nantz was the senior flag officer in the [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]], and was considered to command the enhanced fleet formation [[Black Fleet Crisis|dispatched]] to the [[Koornacht Cluster/Legends|Koornacht Cluster]] in that [[Standard year/Legends|year]]. The fleet's mission was to oppose the [[Speciesism|xenophobic]] [[Yevetha]] after they had conducted [[Yevethan Purge|genocidal]] massacres of [[Planet/Legends|planetary]] populations of [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Species/Legends|species]] within their home region.

==Biography==
{{Quote|Never interrupt your opponent while he is making a mistake.|Firmus Nantz, admiral, New Republic|<ref name="EGTW" />}}
[[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] Firmus Nantz<ref name="EGTW" /> was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]]<ref name="Lando" /> [[Navy/Legends|naval]] [[Officer/Legends|officer]] serving with the [[New Republic Defense Fleet/Legends|New Republic Fleet]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], following the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], Admiral [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]], [[Supreme Commander (Alliance)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] of the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]], reorganized his [[Commander/Legends|command]] into four separate [[Fleet/Legends|fleets]] to better prosecute the war against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the various [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] states that had arisen with the collapse of Imperial command authority in the wake of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Death/Legends|death]].<ref name="EGTW" /> Nantz helped to mastermind the conquest of the Core,<ref name="Insider 2013">{{InsiderCite|Special Edition 2013|10 Great Military Leaders}}</ref> and was given command of the [[New Republic First Fleet|First Fleet]] tasked with operations in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] [[Western Reaches/Legends|Western Reaches]]. Nantz staged his campaign from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Saijo/Legends|Saijo]],<ref name="EGTW" /> in the [[Saijo sector/Legends|Saijo sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="TEA-OC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> From there, Nantz took the First to [[Abraxas]]<ref name="EGTW" /> in the [[Yushan sector/Legends|Yushan sector]]<ref name="TEA-OC" /> where the fleet [[Operation on Abraxas|engaged]] the [[Eiattu pirates]].<ref name="EGTW" />

[[File:Nantz-Calrissian.jpg|thumb|left|Admiral Nantz confers with Lando Calrissian over the Eiattu pirate situation.]]
The [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] were a group of Imperial [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE/IN interceptors]] who were raiding [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] shipping in the region. Over the course of a [[Standard month/Legends|month]], the pirates attacked and captured six [[Convoy/Legends|convoys]], including one bound for [[Bhuna Sound/Legends|Bhuna Sound]]. When a conventional military response proved to be ineffective, Nantz enlisted the help of Alliance hero [[General/Legends|General]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]]. Citing the General's unconventional background, he hoped that Calrissian would be able to succeed where others had failed and gave him carte blanche to assemble a team. Calrissian's unit, known as [[Lando's Commandos]], successfully [[Mission to Radix|tracked]] the pirates to their base on [[Radix]] in the [[Abraxas system/Legends|Abraxas system]]. There, Calrissian employed guile, misdirection, and a [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaked]] [[stealth fighter]] to neutralize the pirate threat and expose one of his team, [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Kine (pirate)|Kine]], as a [[Spy/Legends|traitor]] who had supplied the pirates with information regarding the New Republic convoys. Nantz debriefed Calrissian, commending him on a good job, although the General was saddened over the loss of several personnel during the mission and was glad the mission was over. Nantz offered him the chance to perform other missions, and Calrissian decided to flip a [[Coin/Legends|coin]] to decide his next move.<ref name="Lando" />

The victory at Abraxas was a show of force that was planned to keep two of the new Imperial warlords—[[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Utoxx Prentioch]] and [[Superior General]] [[Sander Delvardus]]—bottled up in their own territories. The tactic succeeded; Prentioch remained in the [[Bomis Koori system/Legends|Bomis Koori system]], while Delvardus guarded the borders of his fiefdom, the [[Eriadu Authority]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> A successful [[Battle of Glova|strike]] by the First at [[Glova]]<ref name="EGTW" /> in the [[Tarabba sector/Legends|Tarabba sector]]<ref name="TEA-OC" />—assisted by [[New Republic Army|ground forces]] commanded by General [[Tyr Taskeen]]—forced Delvardus' military into retreat from the [[Glova system/Legends|system]]. The capture of Glova cut Delvardus off from potential reinforcements from Imperial forces stationed<ref name="EGTW" /> further down the [[Rimma Trade Route/Legends|Rimma Trade Route]]<ref name="TEA" /> in the [[Elrood sector/Legends|Elrood sector]] and [[Minos Cluster/Legends|Minos Cluster]]. Nantz fought and won a [[Skirmish at Kriselist|skirmish]] against Moff Prentioch's forces at [[Kriselist/Legends|Kriselist]]<ref name="EGTW" /> in the [[Wornal sector/Legends|Wornal sector]],<ref name="TEA-OC" /> then bypassed the rest of Prentioch's [[Prentioch's Dominion|territory]] to [[Seizure of Moorja|engage]] Delvardus's forces at [[Moorja/Legends|Moorja]]<ref name="EGTW" /> in the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]].<ref name="TEA-OC" /> During the [[Space warfare/Legends|battle]], a fleet of New Republic [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigates]], supported by [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing bombers]], destroyed the ''[[Thalassa]]'', a [[Praetor Mark II-class battlecruiser|''Praetor Mark II''-class battlecruiser]]. Nantz then moved in force to the [[Hevvrol sector/Legends|Hevvrol sector]]<ref name="EGTW" /> in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]],<ref name="TEA" /> executing a [[Hevvrol Sector Campaign|campaign]] against several targets in the region. A [[Battle group/Legends|battle group]], with [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] operations overseen by Commander [[Adar Tallon/Legends|Adar Tallon]], [[Battle of Bannistar Station|struck]] [[Bannistar Station]], capturing the Imperial [[Space station/Legends|installation]] following a heavy bombardment. An [[Battle of Glom Tho|assault]] on the planet [[Glom Tho]] followed,<ref name="EGTW" /> where New Republic forces decisively defeated an Imperial flotilla<ref name="TEA" /> while General Taskeen's forces [[Ground warfare/Legends|seized]] key foundries.<ref name="EGTW" /> New Republic [[Trooper/Legends|troops]] made extensive use of [[Neuro-Saav Corporation|Neuro-Saav]] [[Sure-Sight]] weapons during the engagement. Glom Tho was the decisive battle in the Hevvrol [[Sector/Legends|Sector]] Campaign,<ref name="SW:RPG2">[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game, Second Edition|''Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game'', Second Edition]]</ref> and the victory blunted the expansion dreams of another warlord, Moff [[Par Lankin]], who ruled the [[Lambda sector/Legends|Lambda sector]].<ref name="EGTW" />

[[File:First_Fleet_advance.jpg|thumb|right|A map showing First Fleet's advances and gains from 4 ABY–6 ABY]]
In [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], Nantz's forces won a relatively simple [[Battle of Yag'Dhul (Galactic Civil War)|victory]] at [[Yag'Dhul/Legends|Yag'Dhul]]<ref name="EGTW" /> over Delvardus's forces, ending the warlord's Coreward expansion,<ref name="TEA" /> which was followed by the [[Battle of Thyferra (5 ABY)|capture]] of [[Thyferra/Legends|Thyferra]]<ref name="EGTW" /> in the [[Jaso sector/Legends|Jaso sector]].<ref name="TEA-OC" /> Nantz's forces subsequently linked up with the rebellious worlds of [[Cilpar]], [[Mrlsst/Legends|Mrlsst]],<ref name="EGTW" /> and [[Bestine IV/Legends|Bestine]] in the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]].<ref name="TEA" /> Entering [[Herglic Space/Legends|Herglic Space]], Nantz opened communications with the native [[Herglic/Legends|Herglics]],<ref name="EGTW" /> who had declared their independence in the post-Endor period.<ref name="TEA" /> While Nantz was positioned in the Inner Rim, Delvardus attempted to [[Battle of Sullust (Delvardus campaign)|retake]] [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]] only to be defeated by elements of the First under the command of a [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]] captain, [[Sien Sovv]]. Nantz dispatched a task force along the Rimma Trade Route to support Sovv and the Sullustans. Delvardus then lost a series of battles along his border<ref name="EGTW" /> at [[Sanrafsix/Legends|Sanrafsix]], [[Kabal/Legends|Kabal]], [[Clak'dor VII/Legends|Clak'dor]], and [[Sluis Van/Legends|Sluis Van]],<ref name="TEA" /> which caused him to abandon his holdings and retreat to the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]]. The [[6 ABY/Legends|following year]], Nantz moved against Prentioch's territory, which resulted in the capture of Prentioch during a [[Siege of Bomis Koori|siege]] at [[Bomis Koori IV|Bomis Koori]]. With the Western Reaches firmly under New Republic control, Nantz began to plan the conquest of the Southern [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core]]. After extensive fighting undertaken by Ackbar's [[New Republic Third Fleet|Third Fleet]], Nantz's forces—along with those under the command of Admirals [[Willham Burke]] and [[Chel Dorat]]—were in a position to advance into the Core. Eventually, the New Republic successfully [[Mission above Imperial City|liberated]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the [[Galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]].<ref name="EGTW" />

[[16 ABY/Legends|Ten years]] later,<ref name="TT">''[[Tyrant's Test]]''</ref> Nantz was promoted<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|359|Nantz, Admiral}}</ref> to senior flag officer of the [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|Combined Defense Forces of the New Republic]]. When the [[Yevetha]]n [[Duskhan League]]—the government of a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Speciesism|xenophobic]] [[Alien/Legends|aliens]] from the [[Koornacht Cluster/Legends|Koornacht Cluster]] in the Deep Core—[[Yevethan Purge|purged]] all non-Yevetha from the worlds of the Koornacht [[Star cluster/Legends|Cluster]] in an act of mass [[Speciecide|genocide]], the New Republic's newly-commissioned [[New Republic Fifth Fleet|Fifth Fleet]] was dispatched to the region to defend New Republic interests in the area. As the [[Black Fleet Crisis|crisis]] escalated, the Fifth was reinforced with task forces pulled from other fleet commands, and Ackbar was forced to replace the current commander, General [[Etahn A'baht]], since the [[Senate Defense Council/Legends|Defense Council]] had lost confidence in his command abilities. Nantz was considered for the role, although Ackbar vetoed his appointment, persuading Alliance hero [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] to accept a [[Commodore/Legends|commodore's]] [[Military rank/Legends|commission]] and command of the force. Solo never reached the Fifth, however; his [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]], ''[[Tampion]]'', was [[Capture of the Tampion|captured]] by Yevetha forces en route to the fleet.<ref name="SOL" /> General A'baht retained command of the fleet, and mused in private with Commodore [[Farley Carson]], of the [[New Republic Fourth Fleet|Fourth Fleet's]] [[Task Force Apex]], that the only reason that neither Ackbar nor Nantz had been sent to assume command of the force was for fear of either of them being taken hostage as well. Despite several setbacks to the campaign, the Yevethan threat was ended by New Republic forces at the [[Battle of N'zoth]], which included the [[Mission to the Koornacht Cluster|rescue]] of Solo from his captors.<ref name="TT" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|War is bloody mathematics seen through to the solution.|Firmus Nantz, admiral, New Republic|<ref name="EGTW" />}}
Cadaverous and stooped,<ref name="EGTW" /> Firmus Nantz was light-skinned and sported a mane of [[Color/Legends|gray]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] that he kept tied back, bushy sideburns that reached down to his cheek, and black [[Eye/Legends|eyebrows]].<ref name="Lando" /> A superb tactician and battlefield commander who effectively commanded fleet elements in combat, Nantz won many significant victories during the New Republic's early [[Standard year/Legends|years]].<ref name="EGTW" /> Nantz was able to accept the reality that conventional military strategy was not the only way to win battles, and had no compunctions about employing unconventional tactics when the need arose.<ref name="Lando" /> By 16 ABY, he was the most senior flag officer serving in the New Republic Defense Force hierarchy, second only to Admiral Gial Ackbar.<ref name="SOL" />

Noted for his brutal honesty, Nantz did not shy away from frank assessments of what he did or the way he achieved it. He believed that an admiral's job was to "industrialize murder at a distance," and told a delegation of neutral planetary leaders as much.<ref name="EGTW" /> His candid nature led the New Republic to keep Nantz away from [[Politics/Legends|politicians]] and civilians as they were afraid that the outspoken Admiral's views about his military operations would be regarded as offensive or insensitive.<ref name="Insider 2013" /> In conversation with General Lando Calrissian following the latter's successful resolution to the Eiattu pirate problem, Nantz noted that casualties were the price the military paid for success when Calrissian lamented on the loss of some of his personnel.<ref name="Lando" /> He also advised to never interrupt the enemy while they were in the middle of committing a mistake.<ref name="EGTW" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Last week's endnotes about Admiral Nantz sparked a lively conversation, one in which it was noted that a distinctive-looking, unnamed officer from 'Lando's Commandos' might make a good Nantz. I ran the idea by Lucasfilm's Leland Chee, who blessed it. So Nantz now has a face.|Jason Fry discusses the process behind the retconning of Nantz into ''Lando's Commandos''|<ref name="Endnotes14">{{Tumblr|subdomain=jasonfry|url=post/26352866819/eg-to-warfare-endnotes-pt-14|name=Jason Fry's Dorkery|text=EG to Warfare: Endnotes Pt. 14}}</ref>}}
Firmus Nantz was first referenced in ''[[Shield of Lies]]'', the second book of ''[[The Black Fleet Crisis]]'' by [[Michael P. Kube-McDowell]] published in [[1996]], merely as "Admiral Nantz." Nantz was referred to again in the third book of the trilogy, ''[[Tyrant's Test]]'', released later that same year. In [[2012]], ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' by [[Jason Fry]] and [[Paul R. Urquhart]] gave Nantz a first name and expanded upon his history as commander of the New Republic's First Fleet.

Nantz's inclusion in ''The Essential Guide to Warfare'' was due to Jason Fry's liking of the ''Black Fleet Crisis'' trilogy. In endnotes written for ''The Essential Guide to Warfare'', Fry likened his caustic personality to that of General [[Wikipedia:William Tecumseh Sherman|William Tecumseh Sherman]], who served in the [[Wikipedia:Union Army|Union Army]] during the [[Wikipedia:American Civil War|American Civil War]]. Fry gave Nantz an expanded role in ''The Essential Guide to Warfare'', involving him in several battles and attributing several quotes to him.<ref name="Endnotes13">{{Tumblr|subdomain=jasonfry|url=post/25851092826/eg-to-warfare-endnotes-pt-13|name=Jason Fry's Dorkery|text=EG to Warfare: Endnotes Pt. 13}}</ref>

In a [http://boards.theforce.net/xentemp/index.php?threads/be-excited-the-essential-thread-to-the-essential-guide-to-warfare.73 thread] on the [[Jedi Council Forums]] on [[TheForce.net|TheForce.Net]], Fry mused that Nantz could be the previously unidentified commander featured in "[[Lando's Commandos: On Eagles' Wings]]," a short [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] written by [[Ian Edginton]] and illustrated by [[Carlos Meglia]] that was published in [[Star Wars Tales 5|''Star Wars Tales'' 5]] by [[Dark Horse Comics]], since both Nantz and Calrissian were involved in battles at Abraxas, and noted that the commander even looked a little bit like how Fry had imagined Nantz.<ref name="Nantz musing">{{JCFCite|url=posts/163443|board=Literature|thread=BE EXCITED: The Essential Thread to The Essential Guide to Warfare|user=[[Jason Fry|jasonfry]]|date=2012-06-25|temp=yes|nobackup=1}}</ref> On [[June 26]], 2012, Fry revealed that he emailed [[Lucasfilm]]'s [[Holocron continuity database|Continuity Database Administrator]] [[Leland Chee]] with his theory, and Chee agreed to [[Retroactive continuity|retcon]] the commander to be Nantz. Fry then posted the confirmation of the retcon in the thread.<ref name="Nantz confirmation">{{JCFCite|url=posts/165664|board=Literature|thread=BE EXCITED: The Essential Thread to The Essential Guide to Warfare|user=[[Jason Fry|jasonfry]]|date=2012-06-26|temp=yes|nobackup=1}}</ref> The information was later included in Fry's ''Essential Guide to Warfare'' endnotes, posted on his tumblr site.<ref name="Endnotes14" />

Nantz was later featured in the article ''[[10 Great Military Leaders]]'' featured in [[Star Wars Insider Special Edition 2013|2013 Special Edition]] of ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'', where ten characters were ranked on their military prowess. Nantz was listed at number five, behind Gial Ackbar, [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]], [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] and [[Xim/Legends|Xim]] the Despot.
